Bash("C:\\source\\erisml-lib\\check_empty_cells.py" << 'PYEOF'
"""
Check all tables in the docx file for cells that are empty but shouldn't be.

For each table, prints:
  1. Table index
  2. The header row text
  3. Any data rows where cells are empty \(row index, non-empty cells, empty columns\)

Only flags "partial data rows" -- rows where at least one cell has content
but another cell is empty. Fully blank rows and header rows are skipped.
"""

from docx import Document

FILEPATH = r"C:\\Users\\abptl\\Documents\\The Geometry of Good - Bond - v1.0.2e - Feb 2026.docx"


def cell_text\(cell\):
    """Return stripped text of a cell, collapsing internal whitespace."""
    return cell.text.strip\(\)


def main\(\):
    print\(f"Opening: {FILEPATH}\\n"\)
    doc = Document\(FILEPATH\)
    total_tables = len\(doc.tables\)
    print\(f"Total tables found: {total_tables}\\n"\)
    print\("=" * 100\)

    tables_with_issues = 0
    total_issues = 0

    for t_idx, table in enumerate\(doc.tables\):
        rows = table.rows
        if len\(rows\) == 0:
            continue

        # --- Determine header row\(s\) ------------------------------------------
        # Use the first row as the header row.
        header_cells = [cell_text\(c\) for c in rows[0].cells]
        num_cols = len\(header_cells\)

        # Collect issues for this table
        issues = []

        # --- Check data rows \(skip row 0 = header\) ----------------------------
        for r_idx in range\(1, len\(rows\)\):
            row = rows[r_idx]
            texts = [cell_text\(c\) for c in row.cells]

            # Normalise to the same column count as header \(python-docx can
            # return merged-cell duplicates; trim or pad as needed\)
            texts = texts[:num_cols]  # trim extras from merged cells
            while len\(texts\) < num_cols:
                texts.append\(""\)

            non_empty = [\(i, t\) for i, t in enumerate\(texts\) if t]
            empty_idxs = [i for i, t in enumerate\(texts\) if not t]

            # Skip fully blank rows \(all cells empty\)
            if not non_empty:
                continue

            # Skip fully populated rows \(no empties\)
            if not empty_idxs:
                continue

            # This is a partial-data row -> flag it
            empty_col_names = []
            for ei in empty_idxs:
                col_name = header_cells[ei] if ei < len\(header_cells\) else f"Col{ei}"
                empty_col_names.append\(f"col {ei} \({col_name}\)"\)

            non_empty_summary = {
                \(header_cells[i] if i < len\(header_cells\) else f"Col{i}"\): t
                for i, t in non_empty
            }

            issues.append\({
                "row": r_idx,
                "non_empty": non_empty_summary,
                "empty_cols": empty_col_names,
            }\)

        # --- Print results for this table -------------------------------------
        if issues:
            tables_with_issues += 1
            total_issues += len\(issues\)

            print\(f"\\nTABLE {t_idx}  \({len\(rows\)} rows x {num_cols} cols\)"\)
            print\(f"  Header: {header_cells}"\)
            print\(f"  Issues: {len\(issues\)} partial-data row\(s\)\\n"\)

            for issue in issues:
                print\(f"    Row {issue['row']}:"\)
                print\(f"      Present : {issue['non_empty']}"\)
                print\(f"      EMPTY   : {issue['empty_cols']}"\)
            print\("-" * 100\)

    # --- Summary --------------------------------------------------------------
    print\("\\n" + "=" * 100\)
    print\(f"SUMMARY"\)
    print\(f"  Total tables scanned : {total_tables}"\)
    print\(f"  Tables with issues   : {tables_with_issues}"\)
    print\(f"  Total partial rows   : {total_issues}"\)
    print\("=" * 100\)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main\(\)
PYEOF)

Bash("C:\\source\\erisml-lib\\check_empty_cells.py" << 'PYEOF'
"""
Check all tables in the docx file for cells that are empty but shouldn't be.

For each table, prints:
  1. Table index
  2. The header row text
  3. Any data rows where cells are empty \(row index, non-empty cells, empty columns\)

Only flags "partial data rows" -- rows where at least one cell has content
but another cell is empty. Fully blank rows and header rows are skipped.
"""

import sys
import io
sys.stdout = io.TextIOWrapper\(sys.stdout.buffer, encoding='utf-8', errors='replace'\)

from docx import Document

FILEPATH = r"C:\\Users\\abptl\\Documents\\The Geometry of Good - Bond - v1.0.2e - Feb 2026.docx"


def cell_text\(cell\):
    """Return stripped text of a cell, collapsing internal whitespace."""
    return cell.text.strip\(\)


def safe\(s\):
    """Make a string safe for printing by replacing non-ASCII chars."""
    if isinstance\(s, dict\):
        return {safe\(k\): safe\(v\) for k, v in s.items\(\)}
    if isinstance\(s, list\):
        return [safe\(x\) for x in s]
    if isinstance\(s, str\):
        return s.encode\('ascii', errors='replace'\).decode\('ascii'\)
    return s


def main\(\):
    print\(f"Opening: {FILEPATH}\\n"\)
    doc = Document\(FILEPATH\)
    total_tables = len\(doc.tables\)
    print\(f"Total tables found: {total_tables}\\n"\)
    print\("=" * 100\)

    tables_with_issues = 0
    total_issues = 0

    for t_idx, table in enumerate\(doc.tables\):
        rows = table.rows
        if len\(rows\) == 0:
            continue

        # --- Determine header row\(s\) ------------------------------------------
        header_cells = [cell_text\(c\) for c in rows[0].cells]
        num_cols = len\(header_cells\)

        # Collect issues for this table
        issues = []

        # --- Check data rows \(skip row 0 = header\) ----------------------------
        for r_idx in range\(1, len\(rows\)\):
            row = rows[r_idx]
            texts = [cell_text\(c\) for c in row.cells]

            # Normalise to the same column count as header
            texts = texts[:num_cols]
            while len\(texts\) < num_cols:
                texts.append\(""\)

            non_empty = [\(i, t\) for i, t in enumerate\(texts\) if t]
            empty_idxs = [i for i, t in enumerate\(texts\) if not t]

            # Skip fully blank rows
            if not non_empty:
                continue

            # Skip fully populated rows
            if not empty_idxs:
                continue

            # This is a partial-data row -> flag it
            empty_col_names = []
            for ei in empty_idxs:
                col_name = header_cells[ei] if ei < len\(header_cells\) else f"Col{ei}"
                empty_col_names.append\(f"col {ei} \({col_name}\)"\)

            non_empty_summary = {}
            for i, t in non_empty:
                key = header_cells[i] if i < len\(header_cells\) else f"Col{i}"
                # Truncate long values for readability
                display_val = t if len\(t\) <= 80 else t[:77] + "..."
                non_empty_summary[key] = display_val

            issues.append\({
                "row": r_idx,
                "non_empty": non_empty_summary,
                "empty_cols": empty_col_names,
            }\)

        # --- Print results for this table -------------------------------------
        if issues:
            tables_with_issues += 1
            total_issues += len\(issues\)

            print\(f"\\nTABLE {t_idx}  \({len\(rows\)} rows x {num_cols} cols\)"\)
            print\(f"  Header: {safe\(header_cells\)}"\)
            print\(f"  Issues: {len\(issues\)} partial-data row\(s\)\\n"\)

            for issue in issues:
                print\(f"    Row {issue['row']}:"\)
                print\(f"      Present : {safe\(issue['non_empty']\)}"\)
                print\(f"      EMPTY   : {safe\(issue['empty_cols']\)}"\)
            print\("-" * 100\)

    # --- Summary --------------------------------------------------------------
    print\("\\n" + "=" * 100\)
    print\(f"SUMMARY"\)
    print\(f"  Total tables scanned : {total_tables}"\)
    print\(f"  Tables with issues   : {tables_with_issues}"\)
    print\(f"  Total partial rows   : {total_issues}"\)
    print\("=" * 100\)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main\(\)
PYEOF)

Bash("C:\\source\\ethicalfinite\\docs\\survey_pe.py" << 'PYEOF'
import sys, io
sys.stdout = io.TextIOWrapper\(sys.stdout.buffer, encoding='utf-8'\)

from docx import Document

DOCX = \(r'C:\\Users\\abptl\\Documents\\Geometric Ethics - The Mathematical '
        r'Structure of Moral Reasoning - Bond - v1.1 - Feb 2026.docx'\)

doc = Document\(DOCX\)
print\(f"Total paragraphs: {len\(doc.paragraphs\)}"\)

# Show everything between Part V heading and Chapter 16 heading
print\("\\n=== Paragraphs between Part V and Ch 16 ==="\)
in_range = False
for i, para in enumerate\(doc.paragraphs\):
    if para.style.name == 'Heading 1' and 'Part V' in para.text:
        in_range = True
    if in_range:
        style = para.style.name
        text = para.text[:100]
        is_bold = any\(r.bold for r in para.runs if r.text.strip\(\)\) if para.runs else False
        is_italic = any\(r.italic for r in para.runs if r.text.strip\(\)\) if para.runs else False
        fmt = ""
        if is_bold: fmt += " [B]"
        if is_italic: fmt += " [I]"
        print\(f"  {i:5d} \({style:20s}\){fmt}: {text}"\)
        if para.style.name == 'Heading 1' and 'Chapter 16' in para.text:
            break

# Also show a few paragraphs of context before Part V
print\("\\n=== 5 paragraphs before Part V ==="\)
for i, para in enumerate\(doc.paragraphs\):
    if para.style.name == 'Heading 1' and 'Part V' in para.text:
        for j in range\(max\(0, i-5\), i\):
            p = doc.paragraphs[j]
            print\(f"  {j:5d} \({p.style.name:20s}\): {p.text[:100]}"\)
        break
PYEOF)

Bash("C:\\source\\ethicalfinite\\docs\\find_index_docx.py" << 'PYEOF'
import sys, io
sys.stdout = io.TextIOWrapper\(sys.stdout.buffer, encoding='utf-8'\)
from docx import Document

DOCX = \(r'C:\\Users\\abptl\\Documents\\Geometric Ethics - The Mathematical '
        r'Structure of Moral Reasoning - Bond - v1.1 - Feb 2026.docx'\)

doc = Document\(DOCX\)

# Find the Index heading
print\("=== Finding Index section ==="\)
index_start = None
index_end = None
for i, para in enumerate\(doc.paragraphs\):
    if para.style.name == 'Heading 1' and para.text.strip\(\) == 'Index':
        index_start = i
        print\(f"Index heading at para {i}"\)
    if index_start and i > index_start and para.style.name == 'Heading 1':
        index_end = i
        print\(f"Next Heading 1 after Index at para {i}: {para.text[:60]}"\)
        break

if index_start is None:
    # Try Heading 2
    for i, para in enumerate\(doc.paragraphs\):
        if 'Index' == para.text.strip\(\) and 'Heading' in para.style.name:
            index_start = i
            print\(f"Index heading at para {i}, style={para.style.name}"\)
            break

if index_start is None:
    print\("Index section NOT FOUND!"\)
    # Search more broadly
    for i, para in enumerate\(doc.paragraphs\):
        if para.text.strip\(\).lower\(\) == 'index':
            print\(f"  Found 'Index' at para {i}, style={para.style.name}"\)

# Show the index structure
if index_start:
    print\(f"\\n=== Index structure \(paras {index_start} to {index_start+20}\) ==="\)
    for i in range\(index_start, min\(index_start + 30, len\(doc.paragraphs\)\)\):
        p = doc.paragraphs[i]
        text = p.text[:100]
        print\(f"  {i:5d} [{p.style.name:20s}]: {text}"\)

    # Find subsection headings within the index
    print\(f"\\n=== Index subsection headings ==="\)
    for i in range\(index_start, min\(len\(doc.paragraphs\), index_start + 2000\)\):
        p = doc.paragraphs[i]
        if p.style.name in \('Heading 2', 'Heading 3'\) and len\(p.text.strip\(\)\) <= 20:
            print\(f"  {i:5d} [{p.style.name:12s}]: {p.text}"\)
        # Stop if we hit the end of the index
        if i > index_start + 5 and p.style.name == 'Heading 1':
            print\(f"  END of index at para {i}: {p.text[:60]}"\)
            break

    # Show the last few paragraphs of the document
    print\(f"\\n=== Last 10 paragraphs ==="\)
    total = len\(doc.paragraphs\)
    for i in range\(total - 10, total\):
        p = doc.paragraphs[i]
        text = p.text[:100]
        print\(f"  {i:5d} [{p.style.name:20s}]: {text}"\)
PYEOF)
